subject: Sports
title: Tour de France: 21 Days of Suffering
description: A fast breakdown of Tour de France: 21 Days of Suffering for builders.

hook.text: 2,200 miles. 21 stages. Mountain passes above 8,000 feet. The Tour de France is the hardest endurance event on Earth.

buildup.text: Created in 1903 to sell newspapers, the Tour became cycling's greatest race—and one of sport's biggest spectacles.

discovery.text: Riders burn up to 8,000 calories per stage and lose up to 10 pounds of body weight during the three-week race.

twist.text: Lance Armstrong won seven Tours—then lost them all when his systematic doping was finally exposed.

climax.text: The race continues to captivate millions. The yellow jersey remains sport's most coveted piece of clothing.

punchline.text: Three weeks of agony for a yellow shirt. That's the Tour de France.

quiz.question: How long is the Tour de France?
